cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how long the Montgomery Bus Boycott lasted. The choices are (A) Less than a year, (B) Less than a month, (C) More than a year, and (D) More than a month. First, I need to go back to the passage provided and look for the dates mentioned. The boycott started after Rosa Parks was arrested on December 1, 1955. The boycott began on that day when the African American community decided to protest by not riding the buses. Then, the passage says that the boycott continued and eventually ended on December 21, 1956. So, if the boycott started in December 1955 and ended in December 1956, that's a full year. Let me count the months. From December 1, 1955, to December 21, 1956, is exactly 12 months. Therefore, the duration is more than a year because it spans from one December to the next.
